\documentclass{article}
\usepackage{booktabs}
\title{Borderless Table Fixture}
\author{Strata Reader Project}
\begin{document}
\maketitle

\section{Introduction}
This document contains a borderless table using the \texttt{booktabs} package and another table based purely on spacing/alignment, which are typical in scientific publications.

\section{Booktabs Borderless Table}
Here is a table showing model evaluation metrics:

\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{lcccc}
\toprule
Model & Precision & Recall & F1-Score & Accuracy \\
\midrule
Strata-Native & 0.9452 & 0.9312 & 0.9381 & 0.9540 \\
Opendata-PDF & 0.8120 & 0.7945 & 0.8032 & 0.8211 \\
Baseline-OCR & 0.8891 & 0.8540 & 0.8712 & 0.8902 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\caption{Model performance comparison on scientific benchmarks.}
\end{table}

\newpage
\section{Aligned Spacing Table}
This table does not use any horizontal or vertical line commands, relying only on alignment:

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{lll}
Item Name & Quantity & Unit Price \\
Standard Widget & 150 & \$2.50 \\
Super Widget & 45 & \$12.00 \\
Mega Widget & 10 & \$99.99 \\
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\end{document}
